commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh The door posts, and the narrow windows, and the galleries round about on their three stories, opposite the door, paneled with wood round about, from the ground up to the windows, and the windows were covered; About. Thus he intimates the inner temple, which was covered with boards. The temple was hidden so far by the chambers; or rather he measured (Calmet) "the ground and up to the windows, which were "shut up with lattices, affording light over the doors (Haydock) and apartments, fifteen cubits from the ground.
